objective: Audit Agent Pulse live counts, monitor visibility, nested commander display, and live runtime accuracy.
consensus_findings: Agent Pulse uses multiple live sources (queue/claimed/results JSON counts, fleet tmux census, commander swarm-state telemetry), but display fidelity has gaps under stale/missing telemetry.
Nested commander progress is visible but compressed and partially hardcoded (e.g., 'full SS-250'), reducing runtime accuracy under non-ideal conditions.
Launcher→monitor handoff has reliability risks (non-atomic fleet write, fallback find glob quoting), which can skew monitor counts.
Smoke tests under-cover pulse realism (no heartbeat/pid/liveness assertions, no real commander execution path), limiting confidence in runtime monitoring claims.
conflicts_or_gaps: docs-claims worker primarily extracted README assertions; code-backed verification came from other workers and is weighted higher.
No direct runtime execution was performed in this squad review; findings are static-audit based.
